116468.explanation: Gemeentebelangen believes that equal opportunities are important, but appointments must be made based on quality. There is only one aspect that Gemeentebelangen includes in its choice: with equal quality, Gemeentebelangen favors someone local first.

116469.explanation: Gemeentebelangen finds that involving residents in major decisions fits with local and independent governance. A referendum could be a means for this.

116472.explanation: Gemeentebelangen opposes patronizing; entrepreneurs know their market best. However, Gemeentebelangen also believes entrepreneurs are responsible that visitors leaving their cafes, restaurants, etc., at very late or very early hours do not cause nuisance to residents.

116473.explanation: Local initiatives for sustainable energy deserve support, as long as they are not prestige projects. This can be financial but does not always have to be. Sustainable action with common sense!

116475.explanation: Gemeentebelangen believes that shelter must take place in the own region! When shelter is needed, it must absolutely be manageable.

116476.explanation: Gemeentebelangen advocates for sufficient trees and greenery, including replanting. Specifically: if trees must be cut down due to unsafe situations (for example at Coevorderstraatweg), this should definitely happen and replanting can take place, preferably nearby.

116483.explanation: Tailored solutions are necessary; sport connects and prevents exclusion, but attention must be paid to the stacking of subsidies.

116484.explanation: The local broadcaster strengthens local democracy and is an independent source of information.

116486.explanation: Gemeentebelangen considers cultural education important, whether music, theater, or anything else, but there are already enough national schemes to make this possible.

116489.explanation: Gemeentebelangen supports increasing safety. Visible enforcement is part of this; it raises safety and works preventively.

116490.explanation: Camera surveillance only where demonstrably necessary, such as in the city center or nuisance locations. We must install higher-quality cameras so that if something happens, the footage is usable.

116491.explanation: Protection against domestic violence is essential, but a women’s shelter does not solve everything. When the request comes from this foundation, we will of course consider it.

116492.explanation: Gemeentebelangen finds this a fine line. It depends on risks; proportionality first! If it turns out that community enforcement officers need this equipment and are well trained to use it, we are in favor.

116497.explanation: The housing shortage is so high that Gemeentebelangen supports investing money in temporary housing for students and young people currently waiting for rental housing.

116498.explanation: There can indeed be reciprocity. Gemeentebelangen Hoogeveen and surroundings believes everyone can contribute to society according to their ability. This also increases the self-worth of residents!

116499.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Hoogeveen and surroundings finds it unacceptable that it is necessary in our country; it should not be needed in a country like the Netherlands! But as long as the national government manages this poorly, we as a municipality must protect and support residents with lower incomes.

116502.explanation: The current policy provides that extra needs are assessed only when requested.

116503.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Hoogeveen and surroundings believes we must remain realistic; we must ensure that roads are safe and clear.

116506.explanation: Care must be at a high level; as a municipality, we meet legal requirements. If it happens to fit, that is fine, but for extra wishes people can look at other solutions than the care provided by the municipality.

116508.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Hoogeveen and surroundings is in favor of tax relief if it is financially responsible.

116513.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Hoogeveen and surroundings is against priority housing for status holders over local home seekers.

116514.explanation: Municipal Interests Hoogeveen and surroundings support increasing the amount of free residual waste disposal.

116515.explanation: Municipal Interests Hoogeveen and surroundings advocate for the protection of valuable agricultural land. We must not become the flat open plain of Drenthe!

116521.explanation: We need to build a lot, especially. Municipal Interests supports a realistic share of social rental housing. Building promotes housing flow, including mobility out of mismatched rentals.

116523.explanation: Municipal Interests Hoogeveen and surroundings oppose splitting houses into separate rooms for rent.

116524.explanation: Municipal Interests Hoogeveen and surroundings consider neighborhood centers and village halls important; these are the heart of the community.

116525.explanation: Municipal Interests Hoogeveen and surroundings believe that we should keep the town center attractive for visitors.

116528.explanation: Municipal Interests Hoogeveen and surroundings favor an independent complaints committee; oversight by such a committee strengthens trust.

116529.explanation: Municipal Interests Hoogeveen and surroundings are against prestige projects, so no costly heat networks without community support.

116530.explanation: Municipal Interests Hoogeveen and surroundings support extra enforcement at known nuisance locations.

116468.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of society. Equal representation of men and women contributes to better decisions and increases residents’ trust in the administration.

116469.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

116472.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have room to run their businesses well. At the same time, neighbors and livability should be considered. Therefore, we believe cafes and restaurants should be able to set their own opening hours within clear agreements.

116473.explanation: Local energy cooperatives ensure residents jointly generate sustainable energy and that the proceeds benefit the local community. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

116475.explanation: People who have fled war and violence have the right to a safe place. The asylum seekers’ center has been part of Hoogeveen society for many years. We believe that reception must be humane and well-organized, with attention to both residents and neighbors.

116476.explanation: Trees provide cooling, clean air, a pleasant living environment, and are indispensable for animals and plants. Therefore, we say: for every tree felled, a new one must be planted. Additionally, we will establish a large new forest near Hollandscheveld (and this will not be the only one).

116483.explanation: Sports keep people healthy and help participation in society. For people on social assistance, sports are often too expensive. We believe sports and culture should be accessible to everyone, with municipal support.

116484.explanation: Local media are very important to know what happens in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to survive. Therefore, we believe the municipality should contribute to strong local journalism.

116486.explanation: Music is an important outlet for children and contributes to their development. Music lessons should not only be available to children with wealthy parents. With subsidies, we provide equal opportunities for every child.

116489.explanation: Everyone should feel safe in their own neighborhood and in the center. More visible enforcement officers on the street contribute to safety and accessibility. It is important they collaborate well with residents and other partners.

116490.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. More cameras (for example in the center) can help with that. Privacy remains important. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene directly and have contact with residents.

116491.explanation: Anyone who does not feel safe at home should always have somewhere to turn. A women's shelter offers protection and peace to those facing domestic violence. Such a facility is indispensable for our municipality.

116492.explanation: Carrying a baton can have a preventive effect. Enforcement officers must be well trained before carrying one. The use of the baton should be a last resort. Therefore, officers must be specially trained in authoritative conduct without using violence.

116497.explanation: Students also have the right to affordable housing. Temporary accommodation can help ease pressure on the housing market. It is important that these homes are of good quality and fit the surroundings.

116498.explanation: People are not on social assistance because they do not want to work. Extra obligations cause more insecurity and do not solve underlying issues. Adding more conditions creates new problems. We choose to provide the right guidance so people gain perspective again.

116499.explanation: If debt remission stops, people with the lowest incomes immediately face problems. They simply cannot afford these costs. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support conditional debt remission.

116502.explanation: At this moment it is not a problem. With the increase in electric cars, it is important that sufficient charging points remain available in the future. This should not come at the expense of regular parking spaces.

116503.explanation: On ring roads, 50 km/h may remain, but in residential areas we want 30 km/h. Lower speeds demonstrably make traffic safer, especially for cycling children and the elderly. It also reduces noise and improves air quality.

116506.explanation: The statement is formulated too generally. Freedom of choice is important, but care must always be appropriate, available, and of good quality. This requires customization and clear agreements.

116508.explanation: Property tax (ozb) is not a knob you simply turn up or down. It pays for important services, while we must also consider affordability for all residents. Therefore, we choose neither a general reduction nor an increase.

116513.explanation: Several groups have statutory priority in allocation of social rental housing, such as those in acute need or with medical reasons. Status holders are included; this is legally established. They are one of the priority groups, not the only one.

116514.explanation: Dumping waste on the street or in nature is unpleasant, unsafe, and unhealthy. By keeping the recycling center free, we encourage residents to dispose of waste properly. This results in cleaner streets and a healthier environment.

116515.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. Hoogeveen already has many solar parks. We have clear rules ('solar ladder') that determine where solar panels may or may not be placed.

116521.explanation: There is a great shortage of affordable rental homes. By building at least 40% social rental housing, we create a better balance in the housing market. This makes affordable living accessible to more people.

116523.explanation: We have a large housing shortage and many people live more spaciously than needed. Allowing the rental of individual rooms can enable more people to find affordable housing. This must be under clear conditions, with good quality and a fair rent.

116524.explanation: Not only all neighborhoods but also all villages must have their own place. We want neighborhood centers to receive more support from the municipality. Previously, we successfully reversed budget cuts to neighborhood centers. We are not called the party of the neighborhood centers for nothing.

116525.explanation: The center must remain easily accessible. We advocate for an off-peak rate (pay less during quiet hours). The first half hour of parking should also be free so that quick errands or library visits cost nothing.

116528.explanation: Independence is very important when handling complaints in youth care. We support an independent complaints committee, provided it aligns well with existing national and regional initiatives. This ensures clear and reliable processing.

116529.explanation: Heat networks can contribute to affordable and sustainable heating of homes. They reduce our dependence on fossil fuels and on countries we do not want to depend on. Therefore, we oppose stopping investments in heat networks.

116530.explanation: Enforcement officers contribute to safety and have a preventive effect. We believe they should be visible not only in Steenbergerpark but throughout the municipality. This increases the sense of safety in multiple locations.

116468.explanation: It is good to have a mix of women and men in the executive board, but all alderpersons must meet the requirements.

116469.explanation: All residents of the municipality must be able to express their opinion. We believe that a referendum is not necessarily the best way.

116473.explanation: We encourage sustainable and independent initiatives.

116475.explanation: The municipality has reached maximum capacity for reception. Closure is not relevant.

116476.explanation: We may cut down trees if safety cannot be guaranteed. If new trees can be planted elsewhere, that is fine.

116483.explanation: Various facilities are available to enable good participation in society.

116489.explanation: This is one of the main priorities of the CDA Hoogeveen.

116490.explanation: This is a key priority of CDA Hoogeveen.

116491.explanation: In Drenthe, the municipality of Emmen arranges this reception on behalf of all Drenthe municipalities.

116492.explanation: The safety of enforcement officers (boa's) is important, but the use of a baton requires careful consideration.

116497.explanation: The municipality of Hoogeveen has relatively few students.

116499.explanation: CDA Hoogeveen is not in favor of generic debt forgiveness. This is tailored and can sometimes be necessary to give people new perspective.

116521.explanation: The number of social rental homes should match the demand in the municipality.

116528.explanation: Every youth care provider is legally required to have an independent complaints committee.

116529.explanation: Not every neighborhood, village or municipality is suitable. Costs, benefits and support must be critically assessed.

116468.explanation: The Local VVD chooses quality and talent. Governance should be based on content, not on quotas.

116469.explanation: The Local VVD believes residents choose at elections. Afterwards, council members make decisions on their behalf.

116472.explanation: The Local VVD stands for freedom for entrepreneurs. Cafés and restaurants decide their own opening hours within reasonable limits.

116473.explanation: The Local VVD finds sustainability important, but it is not the municipality’s task to provide structural subsidies. Projects must be self-sustaining. We help with clear rules and good procedures, not with extra public funds.

116475.explanation: The Local VVD chooses to stop extra reception, but existing reception is not closed hastily without national solutions.

116476.explanation: The Local VVD is for green initiatives, but tailored approaches are needed. Sometimes replanting exactly on the same scale is not feasible or logical.

116483.explanation: The Local VVD finds sport important. Support for children is logical, but for adults, membership of a sports club is not the municipality’s responsibility. We prefer to help people towards work and income.

116484.explanation: The Local VVD finds a local broadcaster important, but extra funding is not automatic. First, the broadcaster must show it works efficiently and truly adds value. We want clear performance and cooperation, no open-ended subsidies.

116486.explanation: The Local VVD encourages culture but opts for tailored solutions. No fixed subsidies for music lessons for everyone, but support where it is really needed.

116489.explanation: The Local VVD wants more enforcement officers (boa’s) on the street for safety and livability.

116490.explanation: The Local VVD deploys camera surveillance in places with nuisance or insecurity.

116491.explanation: The Local VVD believes victims of domestic violence must be safely accommodated.

116492.explanation: The Local VVD wants enforcement officers (boa’s) to be able to do their work safely with good equipment.

116497.explanation: The Local VVD focuses on permanent housing for residents, not temporary student housing.

116498.explanation: The Local VVD believes that those who can work or participate, should do so.

116499.explanation: The Local VVD wants to target assistance. Instead of general debt forgiveness, we choose support that encourages work and reaches those who truly need it.

116502.explanation: The Local VVD wants charging points where they are needed. No fixed standard, but smart choices per location.

116503.explanation: The Local VVD wants tailored measures. Not every street automatically needs a 30 km/h speed limit.

116506.explanation: The Local VVD believes people should be able to choose care that suits them.

116508.explanation: The Local VVD wants to keep housing costs as low as possible. Property tax (ozb) must be lowered where possible.

116513.explanation: The Local VVD believes local residents should not be last in line for housing.

116514.explanation: The Local VVD wants to keep waste costs fair. Free disposal leads to higher charges for residents.

116515.explanation: The Local VVD does not want solar panels on agricultural land. Roofs have priority.

116521.explanation: The Local VVD wants to build what is needed. A fixed percentage of social rental housing does not work.

116523.explanation: The Local VVD wants to limit subdividing homes to keep neighborhoods livable.

116524.explanation: The Local VVD opts for tailored solutions. Not every neighborhood needs a community center.

116525.explanation: The Local VVD wants to keep the city center easily accessible with affordable parking and the first 30 minutes free.

116528.explanation: The Local VVD considers independent complaint handling in youth care important.

116529.explanation: The Local VVD does not want to invest money in heating networks that are not feasible.

116530.explanation: The Local VVD wants extra enforcement in Steenbergerpark for safety.

116468.explanation: The best team consists of both men and women. Men and women complement each other. At the same time, we do not want to set a fixed quota in advance. A municipal executive board may also consist of more women than men.

116469.explanation: The Christian Union considers a referendum unwise. Very few topics are suitable to be decided with a simple 'yes' or 'no'. Residents can give their trust to a representative once every four years. He/she then makes decisions according to conscience.

116472.explanation: It is good for the safety and livability of Hoofdstraat if the opening hours of cafes and restaurants are coordinated.

116473.explanation: The Christian Union promotes sustainable energy and cooperating residents. Support does not always have to be financial but can also consist of advice or guidance.

116475.explanation: The Christian Union believes all municipalities should contribute to asylum reception. Moreover, the asylum seekers center in Hoogeveen functions excellently. There is virtually no nuisance and much voluntary work is done.

116476.explanation: Climate change causes heat stress and water nuisance. Trees protect us from both. Therefore, it is good to at least maintain the number of trees in the municipality.

116483.explanation: People flourish in a community. A sports club can be such a community. For someone with social assistance benefits, this can be very helpful in returning to work.

116484.explanation: The Christian Union considers the Local Broadcaster Hoogeveen (LOH) important because it unites Hoogeveen. We find the current subsidy sufficient to contribute to this.

116486.explanation: Music contributes to the development of every child, both individually and in groups. Therefore, we believe music lessons should be possible for every child.

116489.explanation: Safety and enforcement are concerns for our residents. This is the municipality’s responsibility, together with the police. The Christian Union believes investments should be made in this.

116490.explanation: The Christian Union supports camera surveillance in places where it helps reduce nuisance. There must be a concrete reason. We do not want to use camera surveillance preventively.

116491.explanation: It is important that there is sufficient good care for women whose safety is at risk. The location of the care facility is secondary. It can be in Hoogeveen but may also be in another place in the region.

116492.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) ensure enforcement of the public space. Maintaining public order is a task for the police. Equipping BOAs with batons is therefore not the solution. That responsibility belongs to the police.

116497.explanation: At present there is insufficient demand for student housing. Therefore, it is not logical for the municipality to invest money here.

116498.explanation: Obligations may be attached to social assistance benefits. But these obligations are not an end in themselves. They should help people find a place in society, which can also take the form of volunteer work or informal care.

116499.explanation: The Christian Union supports waiving municipal taxes for residents with a low income. We believe strong shoulders should and can bear the heaviest burdens.

116502.explanation: It is good that we are increasingly switching to electric driving. Not only for the climate but also to be less dependent on oil. Additionally, driving electrically is much cheaper per kilometer than driving on fuel. The municipality has a role to facilitate this transition.

116503.explanation: It is not necessary to drive 30 km/h on all roads within built-up areas. The Christian Union prefers to look at other measures to guarantee safety on roads where 50 km/h is allowed.

116506.explanation: There is a large shortage of healthcare workers. Although we are great supporters of freedom of choice, we do not have the luxury to always be able to realize that.

116508.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is one of the municipality’s sources of income. Useful things are done with that money from which we all benefit. Therefore, we consider lowering the OZB unwise.

116513.explanation: It is important that reception locations for asylum seekers have a flow-through. The only solution for this is that refugees receive priority for social housing.

116514.explanation: We are satisfied with the current 450 free kilograms.

116515.explanation: The Christian Union believes there are sufficient solar parks in Hoogeveen. A new solar park is only acceptable to us if it is owned by a local energy cooperative. Banning them would also exclude local ownership. We do not want that.

116521.explanation: New construction projects must strike a balance between social rental homes, affordable owner-occupied homes, and expensive owner-occupied homes. The balance ensures the best flow-through.

116523.explanation: Renting out a single room can contribute to alleviating the housing shortage. Therefore, we do not want to prohibit this outright.

116524.explanation: All neighborhoods and villages deserve their own community center. This contributes to livability and mutual connection.

116525.explanation: In Hoogeveen you can park very close to the shops. That is a big advantage compared to similar places. We find the parking fees proportional to what you get in return.

116528.explanation: There are currently sufficient options to file or discuss a complaint with the municipality. We also have a municipal ombudsman. We consider that sufficient.

116529.explanation: It is important to eventually stop using gas, if only to avoid dependence on foreign regimes. The construction of heating networks can help with this. The municipality has a role to get this off the ground.

116530.explanation: It is important that residents feel safe when walking on the street. BOAs can assist with this.

116468.explanation: It is good to have an executive board that reflects society. We do not believe this should be achieved through a quota but should be strongly motivated.

116469.explanation: Referendums are a good tool to correct politicians.

116472.explanation: Deciding for yourself ensures that not everyone is on the street at the same time and prevents nuisance.

116473.explanation: Such initiatives are good for society and relieve our grid. They deserve support. This can be done, for example, through an interest-free loan.

116475.explanation: We have a task under the distribution law and must fulfill this task morally and legally.

116476.explanation: At the very least, trees are important for nature and livability.

116483.explanation: There must be no threshold on health.

116484.explanation: This helps to create more connection with the city or village.

116486.explanation: Education has been stripped down for years; we must ensure the best possible offering. We see music education as essential for that.

116489.explanation: This must be and remain a task of the police.

116491.explanation: It is extremely important that women and also men who need this safety can find help in Hoogeveen.

116492.explanation: Violence does not help with safety. The role of the police is not that of the public order officers (boa).

116497.explanation: We must be creative in the housing market; this contributes to that. These homes must be easily accessible by public transport.

116498.explanation: The Participation Act already requires this from people. People with potential must be helped to get the most out of themselves.

116503.explanation: This has proven effective for safety.

116506.explanation: Faith, culture, and belief should not be an obstacle for a care provider. We can take wishes into account but should not enforce them as requirements.

116508.explanation: It is fine as it is.

116513.explanation: This is a national law, and the municipality has no authority over it.

116514.explanation: The current policy was just adjusted; let's evaluate that first.

116515.explanation: We must first cover the roofs (with solar panels).

116521.explanation: There is an extreme shortage; we need to tackle this NOW.

116523.explanation: The subletting to, for example, labor migrants causes many problems.

116524.explanation: This is essential for social cohesion.

116529.explanation: There must be a good alternative on the market. It needs to be affordable, though.

116468.explanation: Aldermen must be appointed based on political quality.

116469.explanation: Power back to the citizen! Only a binding referendum can restore trust in politics.

116472.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be allowed to decide this themselves. Of course, some consideration for the neighborhood is necessary, but usually this can be resolved among parties. If not, we mediate.

116473.explanation: We oppose the fictitious energy transition. No subsidies for unaffordable climate policies.

116475.explanation: Stop mass immigration and local sheltering. Both the asylum seeker center and the temporary housing must be closed as soon as possible, and the asylum seekers must be deported.

116476.explanation: We stand for the greening of the municipality.

116483.explanation: Being a member of a sports club is not a right. We already have too many subsidies. FVD Hoogeveen does believe the entire subsidy system must be revised.

116484.explanation: Not a task of the municipality.

116486.explanation: Music lessons are not a right. However, we find classical education important (also in music). We have other plans to encourage this.

116489.explanation: While we do believe that much more must be done about safety, more public order officers are not the solution. FVD focuses on more police officers and a shift in prioritization. Catch criminals instead of issuing fines!

116490.explanation: FVD Hoogeveen believes that camera surveillance is allowed, but it does pose real dangers to the privacy of Hoogeveners. Therefore, restraint with camera surveillance is our norm.

116491.explanation: Hoogeveen is not large enough to finance this.

116492.explanation: Public order officers have not received sufficient training to patrol with batons. We do not want batons being used on people upset about parking fines. The police must have more possibilities to intervene.

116497.explanation: We build for our own residents. Container homes are bad for the streetscape.

116498.explanation: In principle, we agree, but this must be reviewed on an individual basis to see if it is feasible.

116499.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

116502.explanation: We already have far too many charging points for electric cars in Hoogeveen. We oppose the displacement of the regular gasoline car in society.

116508.explanation: We already pay far too much tax. This can certainly be reduced.

116513.explanation: Our own residents first. Refugees with a residence permit should not be allocated social rental housing at all.

116514.explanation: Lower charges for residents of Hoogeveen. Municipal taxes have already been paid. Therefore, this should be free of charge.

116515.explanation: Stop the landscape-polluting solar panels and the useless energy transition.

116521.explanation: FVD Hoogeveen wants to encourage people as much as possible to buy their own house.

116523.explanation: That is up to the landlords themselves. Of course, a reasonable standard of living must be taken into account.

116524.explanation: While we appreciate neighborhood centers for their unifying role in society, it is simply too expensive and yields too little to open a center in every district. That money can be used more sensibly in other ways.

116525.explanation: FVD preferably wants free parking in the center to encourage visitors and entrepreneurs.

116528.explanation: Youth care is a big mess. Apart from the complaints committee, the functions of all involved agencies must be fundamentally revised.

116529.explanation: Stop the energy transition and the failing climate policy!

116468.explanation: D66 values equal opportunities and representation. A balanced composition fits inclusive politics, although quality remains decisive. 'Must' is a bit too strong an expression.

116469.explanation: D66 supports participation. Sometimes this can be a referendum, but we prefer to look first at citizen panels and consultation processes.

116472.explanation: Entrepreneurial freedom is central, as long as livability and safety are not jeopardized.

116473.explanation: Local energy cooperatives fit with D66 policy for sustainability and citizen initiatives.

116475.explanation: D66 supports humane reception and distribution. Closure without an alternative is not an option.

116476.explanation: Green policies and biodiversity are important. Replanting prevents net loss of trees and contributes to climate adaptation.

116483.explanation: Participation in society and health are important; support can reduce poverty and health problems. D66 is in favor of contributing to this, but it does not have to be the full amount.

116484.explanation: Local journalism strengthens democracy and information provision.

116486.explanation: Education and cultural participation are priorities; equal opportunities for talent development.

116489.explanation: D66 finds safety essential, but first checks if there is actually a shortage of enforcement officers and seeks coordination with the police and them.

116490.explanation: There are already a considerable number of cameras in the city center. Expansion only where demonstrably effective and proportionate. Privacy remains important.

116491.explanation: Protection against domestic violence is essential; safe shelter must be available. If the support services signal a need for such a shelter, the municipality must facilitate this.

116492.explanation: D66 is cautious about arming; emphasis on de-escalation and professional deployment.

116497.explanation: D66 wants to tackle housing shortages with innovative solutions such as temporary construction, provided it fits the environment and local needs.

116498.explanation: Participation is important, but coercion can be counterproductive. Education, guidance, and customization are preferred.

116499.explanation: Protecting vulnerable residents is important; poverty policy belongs to the municipality.

116502.explanation: This aligns with and is part of the municipality's plans regarding the sustainability of mobility and parking.

116503.explanation: This is already common practice in residential neighborhoods. We can consider expansion, but the road must also be designed accordingly. It improves traffic safety and livability in residential areas. For ring roads, 30 km/h is not necessary.

116506.explanation: Person-centered care and freedom of choice are central but within reasonable limits. In emergencies and due to current staff shortages in healthcare, it is not always possible to facilitate all choices.

116508.explanation: D66 chooses a solid budget over a tax reduction. If property tax were to be lowered, choices would have to be made regarding the services the municipality provides. No one likes paying taxes, but lower taxes mean fewer services.

116513.explanation: As with other groups (relationship breakdown, medical reasons), the possibility of priority in housing allocation for status holders is also regulated by law. Moreover, this promotes integration into society and turnover at the reception center.

116514.explanation: Every resident can already dispose of a certain amount free of charge at the recycling center annually, and some materials can always be delivered free of charge. We do prefer low rates as this can reduce littering.

116515.explanation: D66 prefers solar panels on roofs and fallow land first but does not want an absolute ban on solar panels on agricultural land.

116521.explanation: More affordable homes are needed for various target groups. D66 supports mixed neighborhoods with a substantial share of social rental housing. A 40% target rate applies, not an absolute minimum.

116523.explanation: A ban increases the housing shortage. D66 prefers regulation over prohibition.

116524.explanation: Facilities are important, but customization per neighborhood is more efficient than requiring a center everywhere.

116525.explanation: D66 seeks a balance between accessibility, livability, and income. Short-term parking could be cheaper if long-term parking in the center becomes more expensive. We want to focus on better signage to parking lots to better spread the busy times.

116528.explanation: Transparency and legal protection for families are important; independent verification fits with that. D66 considers it important to organize this for Drenthe, not specifically and only for Hoogeveen.

116529.explanation: D66 actually promotes sustainable alternatives to gas. District heating networks may be necessary for the energy transition, provided they are affordable and future-proof.

116530.explanation: The deployment of enforcement officers (BOA’s) must be aligned with concrete nuisances and effectiveness. D66 especially wants to review the layout of the park; more lighting, safe and clear routes; and visibility by trimming back bushes in some places.

116468.explanation: No requirement can be set for an equal male-female distribution; the emphasis should be on content, governance, and responsibility, not on quotas.

116469.explanation: Although we are basically against referendums, we have noticed that for very important decisions with major financial risks, they can be useful.

116472.explanation: The SGP believes that Sunday should remain a collective day of rest and that municipalities should retain the space to regulate opening hours, rather than granting full freedom to entrepreneurs.

116473.explanation: The SGP wants the government and municipalities to invest in sustainability, energy savings, and alternative heat supplies, with attention to affordability for residents. Sustainable energy generation is also part of this.

116475.explanation: The SGP focuses on good safety and enforcement around the asylum seeker center, not on closing it. Partly because it has been manageable to date.

116476.explanation: The SGP emphasizes stewardship and the importance of sufficient public green spaces for health, biodiversity, and livability, which supports planting trees.

116483.explanation: The SGP aims to support vulnerable residents and participation in facilities (such as free swimming lessons for children), additionally for people in poverty but does not want specific policies to pay for everyone’s sports club memberships.

116484.explanation: The SGP wants broad and clear communication from the municipality. So far, the existing budget is sufficient. If more is needed in the future, we will discuss it.

116486.explanation: The SGP wants to support schools in nature and culture education with room for their own interpretation, but does not mention specific subsidies for music lessons for every child, except for children in poverty who otherwise could not take music lessons.

116489.explanation: The SGP wants 15 full-time equivalent enforcement officers available in Hoogeveen, thereby focusing on strengthening supervision and safety.

116490.explanation: The SGP strongly focuses on safety through neighborhood officers, enforcement officers, neighborhood prevention, and supervision, and considers camera surveillance an appropriate means to combat crime. Additionally, it can help trace perpetrators afterwards.

116491.explanation: The SGP wants good protection and support for vulnerable people and families, but does not mention a specific proposal for a domestic violence shelter in Hoogeveen. Additionally, we want to invest much more in prevention to avoid issues.

116492.explanation: The monopoly on violence lies with the government and is primarily exercised by the police. The SGP focuses on sufficient neighborhood officers and enforcement, but further arming of BOA’s can complement a lack of police deployment.

116497.explanation: The SGP wants sufficient building land and diverse housing types to meet the housing needs of residents and workers, which can support temporary student housing.

116498.explanation: The SGP believes those who can work must work and that the right to social assistance lapses for those who can but do not want to work. Additionally, this keeps people on benefits in contact with the business sector, which can positively help them find work.

116499.explanation: The SGP specifically wants to combat poverty and ensure a decent minimum subsistence level for vulnerable households, which supports assistance like exemption from charges.

116502.explanation: The SGP focuses on sustainability and the energy transition with attention to affordability, and with an increase in electric transport it is logical to expand the arsenal of charging stations.

116503.explanation: The SGP emphasizes the importance of traffic safety and good road maintenance. Various studies show that 30 km/h contributes to fewer accidents.

116506.explanation: The SGP wants the right to choose identity-based care to be guaranteed both legally and in practice.

116508.explanation: The SGP is cautious about raising property tax (OZB) and wants part of the leftover money from annual accounts to be used to give a property tax discount in the following year.

116513.explanation: The SGP believes that status holders should be treated equally to ordinary housing seekers when registering for housing and therefore should not receive priority.

116514.explanation: The SGP does want another, more efficient way of waste collection with more reuse and lower costs for residents, but considers it reasonable that a fair amount is paid when using the recycling center.

116515.explanation: The SGP emphasizes careful management of space, nature, and economy from a stewardship perspective. We believe agricultural land is necessary to safeguard our food security.

116521.explanation: The SGP wants sufficient building land and diverse housing types that match residents' needs, but a fixed percentage of social housing is not a solution. It must be a balance of supply and demand.

116523.explanation: The SGP wants room for diverse housing types that match the housing needs of residents and workers, instead of a general ban on room rentals.

116524.explanation: The SGP emphasizes involvement of society and social initiatives. There are already “community centers” everywhere, but for the expected new neighborhoods this must definitely be taken into account in the design.

116525.explanation: The SGP wants not only cheaper but free parking everywhere in Hoogeveen. This will contribute to an increase in visitors, good for retail and hospitality, and a lively shopping center.

116528.explanation: The SGP wants good, accessible youth care with freedom of choice and appropriate support, but does not see a specific independent complaints committee as a solution.

116529.explanation: The SGP sees district heating networks in some neighborhoods as a socially inexpensive solution, provided affordability is well arranged and first clearly mapped out.

116530.explanation: The SGP generally wants sufficient enforcement officers in Hoogeveen (15 FTE). Due to considerable events in Steenbergerpark, adequate enforcement is needed there.

statements.title: The municipality must not invest money in the construction of heat networks.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geen geld steken in de aanleg van

titleParts.glossary: Network of underground pipes that brings hot water from a central source to homes and buildings, for both heating and tap water.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: Hoogeveen should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The residents of Hoogeveen should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Hoogeveen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should set up an independent complaints committee for youth care.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet een onafhankelijke klachtencommissie instellen voor de

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

statements.title: Hoogeveen should get more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The BOAs who monitor safety in Hoogeveen should be issued batons.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Hoogeveen controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Hoogeveen should have a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
At least 40% of the newly built homes in Hoogeveen should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Hoogeveen moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Paid parking in Hoogeveen’s center should be made cheaper.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should deploy more enforcement officers (BOAs) in Steenbergerpark.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.
Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.
